content.scenario: French A1: Greetings and Farewells 🗣️
content.text: In French, how do you say 'Hello / Good day'? {{c1::Bonjour}}
content.explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Greetings and Farewells'. Level: A1.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Hello / Good day' en French? Bonjour
content.usage: Term: Bonjour | Meaning: Hello / Good day

content.scenario: French A1: Greetings and Farewells 🗣️
content.text: In French, how do you say 'Good evening'? {{c1::Bonsoir}}
content.explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Greetings and Farewells'. Level: A1.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Good evening' en French? Bonsoir
content.usage: Term: Bonsoir | Meaning: Good evening

content.scenario: French A1: Greetings and Farewells 🗣️
content.text: In French, how do you say 'Hi / Bye - Informal'? {{c1::Salut}}
content.explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Greetings and Farewells'. Level: A1.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Hi / Bye - Informal' en French? Salut
content.usage: Term: Salut | Meaning: Hi / Bye - Informal

content.scenario: French A1: Greetings and Farewells 🗣️
content.text: In French, how do you say 'How are you? - Formal'? {{c1::Comment allez-vous ?}}
content.explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Greetings and Farewells'. Level: A1.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'How are you? - Formal' en French? Comment allez-vous ?
content.usage: Term: Comment allez-vous ? | Meaning: How are you? - Formal

content.scenario: French A1: Greetings and Farewells 🗣️
content.text: In French, how do you say 'Goodbye'? {{c1::Au revoir}}
content.explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Greetings and Farewells'. Level: A1.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Goodbye' en French? Au revoir
content.usage: Term: Au revoir | Meaning: Goodbye

content.scenario: French A1: Greetings and Farewells 🗣️
content.text: In French, how do you say 'Nice to meet you'? {{c1::Enchanté}}
content.explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Greetings and Farewells'. Level: A1.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Nice to meet you' en French? Enchanté
content.usage: Term: Enchanté | Meaning: Nice to meet you

content.scenario: French A1: Greetings and Farewells 🗣️
content.text: In French, how do you say 'See you soon'? {{c1::À bientôt}}
content.explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Greetings and Farewells'. Level: A1.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'See you soon' en French? À bientôt
content.usage: Term: À bientôt | Meaning: See you soon

content.scenario: French A1: Greetings 🇫🇷
content.text: When entering a French bakery at 7:00 PM, you should greet the staff with '{{c1::Bonsoir}}' instead of 'Bonjour'.
content.explanation: Bonjour is used during the day (Hello/Good morning). Bonsoir is used after 6 PM (Good evening). Saying 'Bonjour' late in the evening sounds unnatural in France.
content.spanish: Al entrar a una panadería francesa a las 7:00 PM, debes saludar al personal con 'Bonsoir' en lugar de 'Bonjour'.
content.usage:
Time guidelines:
  • Bonjour (used from morning until approximately 6 PM)
  • Bonsoir (used after 6 PM)

content.scenario: French A1: Greetings 🇫🇷
content.text: To ask a stranger or colleague 'How are you?' formally in French, you say: '{{c1::Comment allez-vous ?}}'
content.explanation: vous is the formal pronoun of respect. For friends or children, you use the informal 'Ça va ?' or 'Comment tu vas ?'.
content.spanish: Para preguntar formalmente a un extraño o colega '¿Cómo está usted?' en francés, dices: 'Comment allez-vous ?'.
content.usage:
Pronunciation tip: Pronounce the 't' in 'Comment' linked with 'allez' as a 't' sound: Comment allez-vous ? (/kɔ.mɑ̃.t‿a.le.vu/).
  • Formal: Bonjour Madame, comment allez-vous ?
  • Informal: Salut Marc, ça va ?

content.scenario: French A1: Greetings 🇫🇷
content.text: In French, the informal word '{{c1::Salut}}' can be used to say both 'Hi' and 'Bye'.
content.explanation: Salut is a highly versatile informal greeting. It should only be used with friends, family, or people you know well.
content.spanish: En francés, la palabra informal 'Salut' se puede usar tanto para decir 'Hola' como 'Adiós'.
content.usage:
Usage scenarios:
  • Arriving: Salut, ça va ? (Hi, how's it going?)
  • Leaving: Allez, salut ! (Okay, bye!)

content.scenario: French A1: Greetings and Farewells 🗣️
content.text: In French, how do you say 'Welcome'? {{c1::Bienvenue}}
content.explanation: Used to welcome someone to a place. If saying 'welcome' to do something, it is followed by 'de' or 'à'.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Welcome' en francés? Bienvenue
content.usage: Term: Bienvenue | Example: Bienvenue en France ! (Welcome to France!)

content.scenario: French A1: Greetings and Farewells 🗣️
content.text: In French, how do you say 'See you tomorrow'? {{c1::À demain}}
content.explanation: Literally 'until tomorrow'. It is a common parting phrase among colleagues and friends.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'See you tomorrow' en francés? À demain
content.usage: Term: À demain | Example: Bonne soirée, à demain ! (Good evening, see you tomorrow!)

content.scenario: French A1: Greetings and Farewells 🗣️
content.text: In French, how do you say 'Good night' (when going to bed)? {{c1::Bonne nuit}}
content.explanation: Unlike 'Bonsoir' (good evening), 'Bonne nuit' is specifically used right before sleeping or when someone is going to bed.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Good night' en francés? Bonne nuit
content.usage: Term: Bonne nuit | Example: Bonne nuit, fais de beaux rêves. (Good night, sweet dreams.)

content.scenario: French A1: Greetings and Farewells 🗣️
content.text: In French, the formal/polite way to say 'Please' is '{{c1::S'il vous plaît}}', and the informal way is '{{c2::S'il te plaît}}'.
content.explanation: Literally translates to 'if it pleases you'. Match 'vous' or 'tu' depending on the relationship.
content.spanish: En francés, la forma formal de decir 'Por favor' es 'S'il vous plaît' y la informal es 'S'il te plaît'.
content.usage: Formal: Un café, s'il vous plaît. | Informal: Passe-moi le sel, s'il te plaît.

content.scenario: French A1: Greetings and Farewells 🗣️
content.text: In French, how do you say 'Thank you very much'? {{c1::Merci beaucoup}}
content.explanation: The most common way to show high gratitude. Be careful with pronunciation: 'beaucoup' ends with a silent 'p'.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Thank you very much' en francés? Merci beaucoup
content.usage: Term: Merci beaucoup | Example: Merci beaucoup pour votre aide. (Thank you very much for your help.)

content.scenario: French A1: Greetings and Farewells 🗣️
content.text: In French, a standard informal way to say 'You're welcome' is '{{c1::De rien}}'.
content.explanation: Literally means 'of nothing'. More formal versions include 'Je vous en prie' or 'Je t'en prie'.
content.spanish: En francés, una forma estándar e informal de decir 'De nada' es 'De rien'.
content.usage: Term: De rien | Dialogue: - Merci ! - De rien.

content.scenario: French A1: Greetings and Farewells 🗣️
content.text: In French, the informal question to ask someone their name is '{{c1::Comment t'appelles-tu ?}}' or 'Comment tu t'appelles ?'.
content.explanation: Literally 'How do you call yourself?'. The formal equivalent is 'Comment vous appelez-vous ?'.
content.spanish: En francés, la pregunta informal para preguntar el nombre a alguien es 'Comment t'appelles-tu ?'.
content.usage: Informal: Salut ! Comment tu t'appelles ? | Formal: Bonjour, comment vous appelez-vous ?

content.scenario: French A1: Greetings and Farewells 🗣️
content.text: In French, how do you say 'My name is [Name]'? {{c1::Je m'appelle}} [Name]
content.explanation: Using the pronominal verb 's'appeler' (to be called). Literally 'I call myself'.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Me llamo...' en francés? Je m'appelle
content.usage: Term: Je m'appelle | Example: Je m'appelle Jesus.

content.scenario: French A1: Greetings and Farewells 🗣️
content.text: In French, how do you say 'Have a nice day'? {{c1::Bonne journée}}
content.explanation: Used when leaving or saying goodbye during the day to wish someone a good day ahead.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Have a nice day' en francés? Bonne journée
content.usage: Term: Bonne journée | Example: Au revoir, bonne journée ! (Goodbye, have a nice day!)

content.scenario: French A1: Greetings and Farewells 🗣️
content.text: In French, how do you say 'Have a nice evening'? {{c1::Bonne soirée}}
content.explanation: Used when departing in the evening to wish someone a pleasant rest of the evening.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Have a nice evening' en francés? Bonne soirée
content.usage: Term: Bonne soirée | Example: Bonsoir, bonne soirée à tous. (Good evening, have a nice evening everyone.)

content.scenario: French A1: Personal Pronouns and To Be 🗣️
content.text: In French, how do you say 'I am'? {{c1::Je suis}}
content.explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Personal Pronouns and To Be'. Level: A1.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'I am' en French? Je suis
content.usage: Term: Je suis | Meaning: I am

content.scenario: French A1: Personal Pronouns and To Be 🗣️
content.text: In French, how do you say 'You are'? {{c1::Tu es}}
content.explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Personal Pronouns and To Be'. Level: A1.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'You are' en French? Tu es
content.usage: Term: Tu es | Meaning: You are

content.scenario: French A1: Personal Pronouns and To Be 🗣️
content.text: In French, how do you say 'He/She is'? {{c1::Il/Elle est}}
content.explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Personal Pronouns and To Be'. Level: A1.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'He/She is' en French? Il/Elle est
content.usage: Term: Il/Elle est | Meaning: He/She is

content.scenario: French A1: Personal Pronouns and To Be 🗣️
content.text: In French, how do you say 'We are'? {{c1::Nous sommes}}
content.explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Personal Pronouns and To Be'. Level: A1.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'We are' en French? Nous sommes
content.usage: Term: Nous sommes | Meaning: We are

content.scenario: French A1: Personal Pronouns and To Be 🗣️
content.text: In French, how do you say 'You are (formal/plural)'? {{c1::Vous êtes}}
content.explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Personal Pronouns and To Be'. Level: A1.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'You are (formal/plural)' en French? Vous êtes
content.usage: Term: Vous êtes | Meaning: You are (formal/plural)

content.scenario: French A1: Personal Pronouns and To Be 🗣️
content.text: In French, how do you say 'They are'? {{c1::Ils/Elles sont}}
content.explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Personal Pronouns and To Be'. Level: A1.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'They are' en French? Ils/Elles sont
content.usage: Term: Ils/Elles sont | Meaning: They are

content.scenario: French A1: Personal Pronouns and To Be 🗣️
content.text: In French, what is the third-person singular pronoun often used in spoken language to mean 'we' (informal)? {{c1::On}}
content.explanation: Though grammatically singular and conjugated like 'il/elle' (e.g., 'on est'), 'on' is the standard way to say 'we' in conversational French.
content.spanish: ¿Cuál es el pronombre en francés de tercera persona singular usado coloquialmente para decir 'nosotros'? On
content.usage: Term: On | Example: On est fatigués. (We are tired.)

content.scenario: French A1: Personal Pronouns and To Be 🗣️
content.text: In French, what is the infinitive form of the verb 'to be'? {{c1::Être}}
content.explanation: One of the two most important auxiliary verbs in French (the other is 'avoir').
content.spanish: ¿Cuál es el infinitivo del verbo 'ser/estar' en francés? Être
content.usage: Term: Être | Example: Aimer être libre. (To love to be free.)

content.scenario: French A1: Personal Pronouns and To Be 🗣️
content.text: To negate the verb 'être' in French, you wrap the verb with {{c1::ne ... pas}} (or {{c1::n' ... pas}} before a vowel). Example: I am not = {{c2::Je ne suis pas}}.
content.explanation: Standard negation in French uses two particles around the conjugated verb.
content.spanish: Para negar un verbo en francés, se coloca el verbo entre 'ne' y 'pas'. 'Je ne suis pas' = Yo no soy/estoy.
content.usage: Example: Il n'est pas français. (He is not French.)

content.scenario: French A1: Personal Pronouns and To Be 🗣️
content.text: In French, the stressed (disjunctive) pronoun for 'me' (used after prepositions or for emphasis) is {{c1::moi}}, and for 'you' (informal) is {{c2::toi}}.
content.explanation: Stressed pronouns are used after prepositions like 'avec' (with) or 'pour' (for), and in phrases like 'Et toi ?' (And you?).
content.spanish: En francés, el pronombre tónico para 'mí' es 'moi' y para 'ti' es 'toi'.
content.usage: Examples: C'est pour moi ? (Is it for me?) | Je viens avec toi. (I am coming with you.)

content.scenario: French A1: Personal Pronouns and To Be 🗣️
content.text: In French, the stressed pronoun for 'him' is {{c1::lui}} and for 'her' is {{c2::elle}}.
content.explanation: Stressed pronouns are used in compound subjects, after prepositions, or for comparison.
content.spanish: En francés, el pronombre tónico para 'él' es 'lui' y para 'ella' es 'elle'.
content.usage: Examples: Chez lui (At his house) | Avec elle (With her)

content.scenario: French A1: Personal Pronouns and To Be 🗣️
content.text: In French, the stressed pronoun for 'us' is {{c1::nous}} and for 'you' (plural/formal) is {{c2::vous}}.
content.explanation: These are identical to the subject pronouns in form but serve as stressed pronouns in different contexts.
content.spanish: En francés, el pronombre tónico para 'nosotros' es 'nous' y para 'ustedes/vosotros' es 'vous'.
content.usage: Examples: Venez chez nous ! (Come to our place!) | C'est à vous. (It's yours / up to you.)

content.scenario: French A1: Personal Pronouns and To Be 🗣️
content.text: In French, the stressed pronoun for 'them' (masculine/mixed) is {{c1::eux}} and for 'them' (feminine) is {{c2::elles}}.
content.explanation: Make sure to use 'eux' for masculine plural instead of 'ils' after prepositions.
content.spanish: En francés, el pronombre tónico para 'ellos' es 'eux' y para 'ellas' es 'elles'.
content.usage: Examples: Sans eux (Without them - masc) | Pour elles (For them - fem)

content.scenario: French A1: Personal Pronouns and To Be 🗣️
content.text: In French, adjectives following the verb 'être' must agree in gender. For example, if 'He is happy' is 'Il est heureux', 'She is happy' is '{{c1::Elle est heureuse}}'.
content.explanation: Most adjectives add an '-e' for feminine, but '-eux' changes to '-euse'.
content.spanish: En francés, los adjetivos que siguen a 'être' deben concordar en género. 'Il est heureux' (Él está feliz) -> 'Elle est heureuse' (Ella está feliz).
content.usage: Rule: Adjective gender agreement. Example: Elle est heureuse.

content.scenario: French A1: Personal Pronouns and To Be 🗣️
content.text: In French, adjectives following the verb 'être' must agree in number. For example, if 'He is tall' is 'Il est grand', 'They (masc) are tall' is '{{c1::Ils sont grands}}'.
content.explanation: Plural adjectives usually add a silent '-s' at the end.
content.spanish: En francés, los adjetivos que siguen a 'être' deben concordar en número. 'Il est grand' -> 'Ils sont grands' (se añade una 's' muda).
content.usage: Rule: Adjective plural agreement. Example: Elles sont grandes. (They are tall - fem)

content.scenario: French A1: Personal Pronouns and To Be 🗣️
content.text: To ask a question using 'être' in French formally, you invert the subject and verb with a hyphen: 'Are you sure (formal)?' is '{{c1::Êtes-vous sûr ?}}'.
content.explanation: Subject-verb inversion is the most formal way to construct questions in French.
content.spanish: Para hacer una pregunta formal con 'être' en francés, se invierte el sujeto y el verbo con un guion. 'Êtes-vous sûr ?' = ¿Está seguro?
content.usage: Formal question: Est-il là ? (Is he here?) | Es-tu prêt ? (Are you ready? - informal)

content.scenario: French A1: Personal Pronouns and To Be 🗣️
content.text: In French, how do you ask 'Is it ...?' or 'Are they ...?' (often used before nouns)? {{c1::Est-ce que}} or {{c1::C'est}}.
content.explanation: 'C'est' means 'It is'. 'Est-ce que' is a question formula meaning literally 'Is it that...?'
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se pregunta '¿Es...?' o '¿Es que...?' en francés? 'Est-ce que' (para preguntas en general) o 'C'est' (esto es).
content.usage: Example: C'est un livre. (It's a book.) | Est-ce que tu es fatigué ? (Are you tired?)

content.scenario: French A1: Personal Pronouns and To Be 🗣️
content.text: In French, how do you say 'These are / They are' (plural of 'C'est')? {{c1::Ce sont}}
content.explanation: 'Ce sont' is used to identify plural people or things.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Estos son / Ellos son' en francés? Ce sont
content.usage: Term: Ce sont | Example: Ce sont mes amis. (These are my friends.)

content.scenario: French A1: Personal Pronouns and To Be 🗣️
content.text: In French, what is the demonstrative pronoun used to say 'this/that' or 'it' in a neutral way? {{c1::Ça}} or {{c1::Cela}}.
content.explanation: 'Ça' is highly conversational and informal; 'cela' is its formal equivalent.
content.spanish: ¿Cuál es el pronombre demostrativo neutro en francés para decir 'eso / esto'? Ça (coloquial) o Cela (formal).
content.usage: Term: Ça | Example: Ça va ? (How's it going? / Is that okay?) | C'est ça. (That's it.)

content.scenario: French A1: Personal Pronouns and To Be 🗣️
content.text: In French, how do you say 'Who is it?' or 'Who is that?'? {{c1::Qui est-ce ?}} or {{c1::Qui c'est ?}}.
content.explanation: 'Qui est-ce ?' is formal; 'Qui c'est ?' or 'C'est qui ?' is very common in spoken informal French.
content.spanish: ¿Cómo se dice '¿Quién es?' en francés? 'Qui est-ce ?' o 'Qui c'est ?'.
content.usage: Dialogue: - Toc toc ! - Qui est-ce ? (- Knock knock! - Who is it?)
